meta.title: Bill Collection (Insurance Premiums)
meta.catalog: Guidewire BillingCenter — Collections & Delinquency Best-Practice Catalog

steps.output: Arrears record created; queue updated; policy status 'Overdue'
Email and SMS reminder with amount, due date and payment link
Outstanding balance, history and contact preferences verified

steps.activity: Outbound call attempt (if no response by T+5)
steps.output: Max 2 call attempts/day; outcome recorded in CRM

steps.activity: Formal written notice of arrears issued
steps.output: Letter and email: amount, consequences, 14-day cure period
Instalment plan for balances above SGD 500; approved by Credit Investigator
Escalation at T+21; investigator intensifies outreach
Formal lapse notice per SOP-INS-011; 7-day final cure period
Payment applied; status reset 'Active'; reinstatement endorsement if lapsed

steps.activity: No payment — policy lapsed, coverage ceased
steps.output: Policy lapsed per SOP-INS-011; policyholder notified; balance referred to Credit
Debt file passed to approved external agency; internal case closed
Write-off request to Finance Director; GL adjustment processed

swim_phases.name: Initial arrears
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–4 · T+1 to T+7
swim_phases.note: Missed payments are flagged automatically; reminders precede analyst review and outbound calls.

swim_phases.name: Escalated arrears
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 5–8 · T+8 to T+30
swim_phases.note: Unresolved arrears escalate to formal notice, hardship plans, and a final lapse notice at T+30.

swim_phases.name: Resolution or write-off
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 9–12 · T+31 onwards
swim_phases.note: Paid cases are reinstated; unpaid balances lapse, route to an external agency, and are written off at T+90.

fitgap.summary_line: Overall alignment of SOP-INS-003 against the Guidewire BillingCenter collections catalog

items.name: Automated arrears identification
items.detail: Billing-system arrears flag with status update aligns with BillingCenter delinquency-plan entry.

items.name: Multi-channel reminders
items.detail: Automated email and SMS reminders with a payment link align with BillingCenter notification templates.

items.name: Proactive pre-due reminders
items.detail: Reminders only start T+1 after a miss. BillingCenter recommends pre-due nudges to prevent arrears entirely.

items.name: Risk-based collections segmentation
items.detail: Every account follows the same T+1/T+5/T+8 milestones. BillingCenter prioritises treatment by propensity-to-pay and balance, focusing effort where recovery is likely.

items.name: Formal arrears notice & cure period
items.detail: Written notice with a 14-day cure period aligns with BillingCenter dunning-letter cadence.

items.name: Payment-plan / arrangement
items.detail: Instalment plans are offered but only via an analyst. BillingCenter supports self-service arrangements and automated promise-to-pay tracking with break-detection.

items.name: Escalation thresholds
items.detail: T+21 escalation to an investigator aligns with BillingCenter delinquency-stage transitions.

items.name: Hardship & forbearance
items.detail: Hardship plans exist but without a documented forbearance/affordability framework or regulatory hardship evidence capture recommended by the catalog.

items.name: Lapse / reinstatement
items.detail: Lapse notice and reinstatement endorsement align with BillingCenter cancellation-for-non-payment handling.

items.name: Partial-payment allocation
items.detail: No documented allocation of partial payments to oldest arrears. BillingCenter applies a configurable allocation hierarchy automatically.

items.name: External agency referral
items.detail: Threshold-based external referral with file hand-off aligns with BillingCenter agency-placement.

items.name: Bad-debt provisioning (ECL)
items.detail: Write-off is manual at T+90 with no automated IFRS 9 expected-credit-loss provisioning through the ageing. BillingCenter feeds provisioning models from delinquency stage.

items.name: Write-off governance
items.detail: Write-off approval to the DOA matrix with GL adjustment aligns with BillingCenter write-off controls.

critical_missing.title: Risk-based collections segmentation
critical_missing.detail: All accounts follow identical milestones regardless of balance or propensity-to-pay. The catalog prioritises treatment by recovery likelihood, improving yield and cutting cost-to-collect.

critical_missing.title: Proactive pre-due reminders
critical_missing.detail: Outreach starts only after a missed payment. Pre-due nudges in the catalog prevent avoidable arrears.

critical_missing.title: Self-service arrangements & promise-to-pay
critical_missing.detail: Payment plans require an analyst; there is no digital self-service arrangement or automated promise-to-pay tracking with break detection.

critical_missing.title: Automated bad-debt provisioning (IFRS 9 ECL)
critical_missing.detail: Provisioning is manual at write-off; the catalog feeds expected-credit-loss provisioning from delinquency stage for accurate, timely reserves.

control_bars.name: Arrears detection
control_bars.note: Automated overdue flag and status update

control_bars.name: Arrangements
control_bars.note: Analyst-only; no self-service or PTP tracking

control_bars.name: Lapse / reinstatement
control_bars.note: Lapse notice and reinstatement well defined

control_bars.name: Provisioning
control_bars.note: Manual at write-off; no ECL feed

control_bars.name: Write-off governance
control_bars.note: DOA approval and GL posting solid

remediations.title: Introduce risk-based collections segmentation
remediations.detail: Score arrears by propensity-to-pay and balance; assign differentiated treatment paths so effort concentrates where recovery is likely. Effort: 5 weeks (model + workflow).

remediations.title: Add proactive pre-due reminders
remediations.detail: Send pre-due nudges (T-5/T-2) via SMS and email to prevent avoidable arrears. Effort: 2 weeks.

remediations.title: Launch self-service arrangements & promise-to-pay
remediations.detail: Let policyholders self-serve a payment arrangement online with automated promise-to-pay tracking and break detection. Effort: 6 weeks (portal).

remediations.title: Automate IFRS 9 ECL provisioning
remediations.detail: Feed expected-credit-loss provisioning from delinquency stage and ageing, replacing manual write-off-only provisioning. Effort: 4 weeks (Finance + Sapiens).

remediations.title: Add partial-payment allocation & forbearance framework
remediations.detail: Apply a configurable allocation hierarchy to partial payments and document a regulatory forbearance/affordability framework for hardship. Effort: 3 weeks.

opt_steps.output: Pre-due nudges (T-5/T-2) by SMS and email to prevent arrears
Account scored by propensity-to-pay and balance; differentiated path assigned
Channel and cadence tailored to the assigned segment
Online arrangement with automated promise-to-pay tracking and break detection
Lapse notice per SOP-INS-011; 7-day final cure
Policy lapsed; balance referred to Credit
Expected-credit-loss provision updated from delinquency stage and ageing
Debt file to external agency; internal case closed
Write-off to Finance Director per DOA; GL adjustment

opt_swim_phases.name: Prevent & detect arrears
opt_swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–5 · v2.0 adds prevention & segmentation
opt_swim_phases.note: v2.0 adds pre-due reminders and risk-based segmentation so collection effort is prioritised by recovery likelihood.

opt_swim_phases.name: Escalated arrears
opt_swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 6–9 · self-service arrangements
opt_swim_phases.note: Policyholders can self-serve a payment arrangement online; promise-to-pay is tracked automatically with break detection.

opt_swim_phases.name: Resolution, write-off & provisioning
opt_swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 10–14 · v2.0 adds ECL provisioning
opt_swim_phases.note: v2.0 feeds expected-credit-loss provisioning from delinquency stage so reserves stay accurate ahead of any write-off.

optimised_doc.subtitle: Optimised per Fit-Gap Analysis against the Guidewire BillingCenter Collections & Delinquency Catalog

blocks: OPTIMISATION SUMMARY — What changed in v2.0

This version incorporates 5 remediation actions from Fit-Gap Report FG-INS-003. Alignment to the Guidewire BillingCenter collections catalog increased from 68% to 90%. Critical additions: proactive pre-due reminders, risk-based collections segmentation, self-service payment arrangements with promise-to-pay tracking, automated IFRS 9 ECL provisioning, and partial-payment allocation.
NEW STEP (green) — step added to close a gap vs the BillingCenter catalog
ENHANCED (amber) — existing step updated with additional controls or automation

sections.title: 1. Purpose
blocks: This Standard Operating Procedure defines the process for collecting outstanding premiums from individual and commercial policyholders. Version 2.0 incorporates optimisations from fit-gap analysis against the Guidewire BillingCenter Collections & Delinquency Best-Practice Catalog, achieving 90% alignment (up from 68% in v1.3).

sections.title: 2. Scope
blocks: This procedure applies to overdue instalments (30/60/90-day arrears), dishonoured direct debits and returned cheques, and reinstatement premium collection following a lapse notice.
Scope extended: pre-due reminders and risk-based segmentation now apply to every billed account before it falls into arrears.
This procedure does NOT cover employee expense reimbursements, reinsurance premium disputes, or broker commission clawbacks.

sections.title: 4. Roles and Responsibilities
blocks: Works the segmented arrears queue; outbound contact; records outcomes; processes inbound payments.
Handles complex/high-value arrears; disputes; reinstatement coordination.
Approves arrangements; owns ECL provisioning inputs, agency referral and write-off recommendations.
Supports outreach for their book; flags hardship; facilitates arrangements.

sections.title: 5. Prevention & Segmentation (New Section — v2.0)
blocks: New section. Required by the BillingCenter catalog. Pre-due reminders and propensity-based segmentation now precede any arrears milestone.

sections.title: 7. Exception Handling
blocks: Analyst escalates the dispute to a Collections Investigator within 1 business day.
Investigator reviews records and responds within 5 business days; collections suspended pending resolution.
Valid disputes: billing corrected and an adjusted statement issued.
Hardship now follows a documented forbearance/affordability framework with evidence capture.
Policyholder submits a hardship application; Credit Investigator assesses within 3 business days.
Extended plan up to 6 months approved; only timing is adjusted — total premium is not reduced.
Forbearance terms and affordability evidence are recorded for regulatory audit.
Collections activity suspended immediately on notification of death.
Case transferred to Claims (estate recovery) and Legal; no external agency referral.

sections.title: 8. Systems and Tools
blocks: Outbound call management and compliance logging
ECL provisioning, write-off postings, bad-debt provisions
Debt file upload and status tracking

sections.title: 9. Key Controls and Audit Points
blocks: Controls expanded in v2.0: pre-due prevention, propensity segmentation, self-service arrangements, and automated ECL provisioning.
Prevention: pre-due reminders reduce avoidable arrears entry.
Segmentation: treatment prioritised by propensity-to-pay and balance.
Promise-to-pay: arrangements tracked automatically with break detection.
Provisioning: IFRS 9 ECL updated from delinquency stage, not only at write-off.
Allocation: partial payments applied to oldest arrears by a configurable hierarchy.
Forbearance: hardship terms and affordability evidence documented.
Write-off governance: DOA approval with GL posting and immutable audit trail.

sections.title: 11. Document Control
blocks: Added deceased policyholder exception; aligned write-off to DOA matrix
OPTIMISED: pre-due reminders; risk-based segmentation; self-service arrangements & promise-to-pay; IFRS 9 ECL provisioning; partial-payment allocation; catalog alignment 68% → 90%

hierarchy_cover_sub: Phase breakdown with arrears milestones, escalation authority, and exception paths
